report.defect_summary: [assignment][P0] Fix run times out and blocks release while live 8090 still reproduces DTS-00001
report.report_text:
Module: Defect process chain > assignment execution / release handoff
Suggested Priority: P0

Phenomenon:
- Fix node dr-20260327-fc1b947def-fix ran as arun-20260327-225945-80da1b and ended with status=failed and failure_reason=assignment execution timeout after 1200s.
- Runtime events during the same run showed code repair steps and a local task-execute smoke passing before timeout.
- Immediately after the timeout, a fresh black-box retest on live 8090 still reproduced the original failure in session sess-web-20260327-232020-a7e191 task task-20260327-d8b10e5f.
- Release node remained blocked by upstream failure, so the fix chain could not close.

Expected Result:
- A fix node that reaches repair and local verification should either complete successfully and hand off to release, or fail fast with a concrete implementation/test error.
- The release node should not be left blocked by an opaque assignment timeout after fix work has already progressed.

Actual Result:
- Fix execution ended with assignment timeout after 1200s at 2026-03-27T23:19:47+08:00.
- Release stayed blocked because the upstream fix node failed.
- Live 8090 runtime still reproduced DTS-00001 after the timed-out fix attempt.

Impact:
- The remediation pipeline cannot reliably push a verified fix through release.
- A P0 production bug remains unresolved even after the automated fix chain spent time applying and validating changes.

Evidence:
- D:\code\AI\J-Agents\Analyst\.test\20260327-231844-225\artifacts\99-summary.json
- D:\code\AI\J-Agents\Analyst\.test\20260327-231844-225\artifacts\02-status-detail-latest.json
- D:\code\AI\J-Agents\Analyst\.test\20260327-232020-167\artifacts\99-summary.json
- D:\code\AI\J-Agents\Analyst\.test\20260327-232020-167\artifacts\06-task-trace.json
- D:\code\AI\J-Agents\Analyst\.test\20260327-232518-310\artifacts\01-release-status.json
